name: Rosemary Chicken and Potato Pizza
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon chopped fresh thyme leaves (or 1/2 teaspoon dried)
1 teaspoon chicken base or bouillon cube (no MSG)
1/2 pound small red "new" potatoes, sliced into 1/8-inch-thick rounds
1 teaspoon chopped fresh oregano leaves (or 1/2 teaspoon dried)
2 teaspoons chopped fresh rosemary leaves (or 1 teaspoon dried)
Cornmeal, semolina or flour for handling
2 teaspoons chopped fresh rosemary leaves (or 1 teaspoon dried)
3 teaspoons chopped fresh oregano leaves (or 1 1/2 teaspoons dried)
recipeInstructions:
To make Garlic-Shallot Butter with Lemon:
1. Melt 5 tablespoons butter in a nonstick saucepan over medium-high heat.
2. Add shallot, garlic, and thyme. Cook, stirring until the mixture is light brown, about 7 to 8 minutes.
3. Stir in salt, pepper, wine, lemon juice, and chicken base. Cook until the mixture reduces to about 1/2 cup (toward the end of reduction, reduce heat to low and stir frequently to prevent scorching).
4. Remove from heat; whisk remaining butter into the sauce.
To make Rosemary Potatoes:
5. Preheat oven to 325°F.
6. Combine sliced potatoes with minced garlic, oregano, rosemary, salt, and olive oil. Spread on a sheet pan in a single layer (do not overlap). Discard marinade.
7. Cook for approximately 45 minutes, turning slices occasionally until browned. Remove from oven; let cool on paper towels.
To make Grilled Garlic Chicken:
8. Prepare grill.
9. Combine minced garlic, soy sauce, salt, and olive oil. Marinate chicken breasts in the garlic oil for about 15 minutes.
10. Grill chicken for 5 to 7 minutes per side until cooked through. Remove from grill and let cool thoroughly. Cut into cubes and refrigerate.
To make the pizza:
11. Place pizza stone in oven center; preheat to 500°F for one hour before cooking.
12. Spread 2 tablespoons Garlic-Shallot Butter on dough surface within rim.
13. Cover with half mozzarella and evenly distribute grilled chicken over cheese.
14. Sprinkle half rosemary and oregano over chicken. Place potatoes over toppings, spaced about 1 inch apart.
15. Transfer to oven; bake until crust is crisp and golden, and cheese at center is bubbly, about 8 to 10 minutes. Remove from oven and sprinkle remaining parsley over hot potato topping. Slice and serve.
Repeat with remaining ingredients for second pizza (pizzas can be prepared simultaneously if placed carefully on opposite corners of the stone).
